action.hypothesis: A factorized nonlinear encoder may lower FVU at K=128 by adding a hidden mixing stage before latent scoring, giving the encoder more expressive feature composition while keeping the fixed-K decode path unchanged. Test factorized_topk at the same K=128 anchor point as the next quality-first family.
action.summary: Both mixture_topk and residual_topk archived under normal runtime with proxy FVU near the topk proxy anchor, so the next move is another architecture family rather than smaller-K search. factorized_topk is the clearest remaining low-friction expressivity upgrade in the current codebase.
action.self_review: This is still exploratory, but it continues the right search pattern: hold K fixed, change one architectural idea, and see whether encoder expressivity can move proxy FVU. If this also comes back flat, the current built-in families are not yet showing a path to the target.
notes_to_memory: residual_topk at K=128/ef8/signum archived under normal runtime with proxy FVU 0.3055884689092636, so the residual two-branch prototype did not improve on the anchor.
Round 4 opens factorized_topk as the next expressive family to test at fixed K=128 before considering lower-K or cost-driven moves.
next_hypotheses: If factorized_topk improves proxy FVU, follow with a stabilization pass on optimizer or expansion factor before promotion.
If factorized_topk is also flat or worse, shift from parameter-only family swapping to a code edit that introduces a genuinely new encoder family or materially changes one incubating family.
